channel.title: NPR Topics: Health
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Just gave birth or are about to? Share your story and questions about costs
item.description: The costs of having a baby in the U.S. can be hard to predict and budget for. We want to hear your stories and questions about how to navigate the system.
item.content:encoded: The costs of having a baby in the U.S. can be hard to predict and budget for. We want to hear your stories and questions about how to navigate the system.

item.title: Former USAID head grieves its closure while hoping for its future
item.description: Former USAID administrator reflects on a year since USAID's shutdown and the new direction of US foreign aid.
item.content:encoded:
Former USAID administrator reflects on a year since USAID's shutdown and the new direction of US foreign aid.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Fatma Tanis

item.title: Oregon ER doctors win a 'David and Goliath' battle against a national company
item.description: In a test of a new state law, doctors in Eugene went up against a national physician staffing firm seeking to replace them. Their success is getting attention across the U.S. as other states consider similar laws.
item.content:encoded:
In a test of a new state law, doctors in Eugene went up against a national physician staffing firm seeking to replace them. Their success is getting attention across the U.S. as other states consider similar laws.

(Image credit: sorbetto/Digital Vision Vectors)
item.dc:creator: Alex Olgin

item.title: Going outside in this heat? Follow these guidelines to stay safe
item.description: Man, it's a hot one! Don't go out in this summer's heat wave before you arm yourself with these tips and a really big water bottle.
item.content:encoded:
Man, it's a hot one! Don't go out in this summer's heat wave before you arm yourself with these tips and a really big water bottle.

(Image credit: SelÃ§uk Acar/Anadolu)
item.dc:creator: Carmel Wroth

item.title: The U.S. healthcare system is in crisis. A Supreme Court ruling could make things worse
item.description: Mass deportations would be felt across hospitals and emergency rooms, which already face persistent staffing shortfalls. The long-term healthcare sector will suffer the greatest disruptions, experts say.
item.content:encoded:
Mass deportations would be felt across hospitals and emergency rooms, which already face persistent staffing shortfalls. The long-term healthcare sector will suffer the greatest disruptions, experts say.

(Image credit: Spencer Platt)
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Romo

item.title: Federal civil rights data holds schools accountable. Under Trump, it's 6 months late
item.description: The Education Department has long collected civil rights data about things like bullying, harassment and disability services in schools, but it hasn't made the latest information public.
item.content:encoded: The Education Department has long collected civil rights data about things like bullying, harassment and disability services in schools, but it hasn't made the latest information public.
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: As a heat wave grips the U.S., a look at how extreme heat affects the body
item.description: As heat waves sweep the country, NPR's A Martinez asks W. Larry Kenney, professor of physiology and kinesiology at Penn State University, how extreme heat affects humans.
item.content:encoded: As heat waves sweep the country, NPR's A Martinez asks W. Larry Kenney, professor of physiology and kinesiology at Penn State University, how extreme heat affects humans.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Lone star ticks are covering much of the U.S. Here's what you need to know
item.description: It's a tick that hunts you down and transmits a potentially dangerous allergy to red meat. The New Yorker writer Burkhard Bilger discusses the lone star tick and the risks of alpha-gal syndrome.
item.content:encoded: It's a tick that hunts you down and transmits a potentially dangerous allergy to red meat. The New Yorker writer Burkhard Bilger discusses the lone star tick and the risks of alpha-gal syndrome.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: 5 safety tips to keep you out of the emergency room this summer
item.description: Experts share guidance on how to prevent common summertime health risks, such as drowning, fire accidents and heat exhaustion.
item.content:encoded: Experts share guidance on how to prevent common summertime health risks, such as drowning, fire accidents and heat exhaustion.
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: Kids with autism are prone to drowning. Florida is trying to prevent that
item.description: Kids with autism can be 160 times more likely than other children to drown. Florida is dedicating state money to a program that prioritizes swim lessons for these kids.
item.content:encoded:
Kids with autism can be 160 times more likely than other children to drown. Florida is dedicating state money to a program that prioritizes swim lessons for these kids.

(Image credit: LA Johnson)
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta
